chapters.name: Triangles
topics: Definitions, examples, counter examples of similar triangles
Proof: If a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle to intersect the other two sides in distinct points, the other two sides are divided in the same ratio
Proof: If a line divides any two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, the line is parallel to the third side
Proof: If in two triangles, corresponding angles are equal, their corresponding sides are proportional and the triangles are similar
Proof: If the corresponding sides of two triangles are proportional, their corresponding angles are equal and the two triangles are similar
Proof: If one angle of a triangle is equal to one angle of another triangle and the sides including these angles are proportional, the two triangles are similar

Proof: If a perpendicular is drawn from the vertex of the right angle of a right triangle to the hypotenuse, the triangles on each side of the perpendicular are similar to the whole triangle and to each other
Proof: The ratio of the areas of two similar triangles is equal to the ratio of the squares of their corresponding sides
Proof: In a right triangle, the square on the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares on the other two sides
Proof: In a triangle, if the square on one side is equal to sum of the squares on the other two sides, the angles opposite to the first side is a right angle

topics: Tangent to a circle at point of contact

Integration by substitution, by partial fractions and by parts
Evaluation of simple integrals of standard types
Basic properties of definite integrals and evaluation of definite integrals

chapters.name: Application of the Integrals
topics: Finding the area under simple curves, especially lines, circles/parabolas/ellipses (in standard form only)

chapters.name: Differential Equations
topics: Definition, order and degree, general and particular solutions of a differential equation
Solution of differential equations by method of separation of variables
Solutions of homogeneous differential equations of first order and first degree
Solutions of linear differential equations of standard types

chapters.name: Vectors
topics: Vectors and scalars, magnitude and direction of a vector
Direction cosines and direction ratios of a vector
Types of vectors (equal, unit, zero, parallel and collinear vectors)
Position vector of a point, negative of a vector, components of a vector
Addition of vectors, multiplication of a vector by a scalar
Position vector of a point dividing a line segment in a given ratio
Definition, Geometrical Interpretation, properties and application of scalar (dot) and vector (cross) product of vectors

chapters.name: Three-dimensional Geometry
topics: Direction cosines and direction ratios of a line joining two points
Cartesian equation and vector equation of a line
Skew lines, shortest distance between two lines

chapters.name: Linear Programming
topics: Introduction, related terminology (constraints, objective function, optimization)
Graphical method of solution for problems in two variables
Feasible and infeasible regions (bounded or unbounded)

chapters.name: Probability
topics: Random variable and its probability distribution

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 1: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
topics: development of male and female gametophytes
fertilization events - development of endosperm and embryo

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 2: Human Reproduction
topics: microscopic anatomy of testis and ovary

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 3: Reproductive Health
topics: Need for reproductive health and prevention of Sexually Transmitted Diseases (STDs)
contraception and medical termination of pregnancy (MTP)
infertility and assisted reproductive technologies - IVF

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 4: Principles of Inheritance and Variation
topics: multiple alleles and inheritance of blood groups

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 5: Molecular Basis of Inheritance
topics: Search for genetic material and DNA as genetic material

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 6: Evolution
topics: biological evolution and evidences for biological evolution (paleontology
mechanism of evolution - variation (mutation and recombination)

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 7: Human Health and Diseases
Biology Chapter 8: Microbes in Human Welfare
topics: microbes as bio-control agents and bio-fertilizers. Antibiotics

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 9: Biotechnology - Principles and Processes
Biology Chapter 10: Biotechnology and its Applications
topics: Application of biotechnology in health and agriculture: Human insulin and vaccine production

chapters.name: Biology Chapter 13: Biodiversity and its Conservation
Chemistry Unit 1: Solutions
topics: expression of concentration of solutions of solids in liquids
determination of molecular masses using colligative properties

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 2: Electrochemistry
topics: Relation between Gibbs energy change and EMF of a cell
dry cell-electrolytic cells and Galvanic cells

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 3: Chemical Kinetics
topics: Rate of a reaction (Average and instantaneous)
order and molecularity of a reaction
integrated rate equations and half-life (only for zero and first order

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 4: d and f Block Elements
topics: general trends in properties of the first row transition metals – metallic
oxidation states and comparison with lanthanides

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 5: Coordination Compounds
topics: IUPAC nomenclature of mononuclear coordination compounds.

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 6: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
topics: optical rotation mechanism of substitution reactions.

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 7: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
topics: uses with special reference to methanol and ethanol.

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
topics: reactivity of alpha hydrogen in aldehydes

chapters.name: Chemistry Unit 9: Amines
topics: chemical reactions and importance in synthetic organic

topics: Coulomb's law-force between two- point charges
superposition principle and continuous charge distribution.
electric field due to a point charge
torque on a dipole in uniform electric field.
statement of Gauss's theorem and its applications to find field due to infinitely
uniformly charged infinite plane sheet and uniformly charged thin spherical

topics: electric potential due to a point charge
electrical potential energy of a system of two-point
charges and of electric dipole in an electrostatic field.
free charges and bound charges inside a conductor. Dielectrics
combination of capacitors in series and
capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor with and without dielectric medium
energy stored in a capacitor (no derivation

chapters.name: Physics Chapter 3: Current Electricity
topics: flow of electric charges in a metallic conductor
potential difference and emf of a cell
of cells in series and in parallel

topics: Biot - Savart law and its application to current carrying circular loop.
Ampere's law and its applications to infinitely long straight wire. Straight solenoid (only

topics: bar magnet as an equivalent solenoid (qualitative treatment only)
intensity due to a magnetic dipole (bar magnet) along its axis and perpendicular to its axis
torque on a magnetic dipole (bar magnet) in a uniform magnetic
dia- and ferro – magnetic substances with examples
effect of temperature on magnetic properties.

topics: peak and RMS value of alternating current/voltage

topics: gamma rays) including elementary facts about their uses.

topics: combination of thin lenses in contact
